target.title: feat(telegram): opt-in interleaved progress lane
target.body:
## Summary

Add an opt-in Telegram renderer that projects reasoning text and structured runtime events into a single durable live message on the reasoning lane, matching CLI ergonomics without changing execution semantics.

**Gated on three conditions:** `toolProgressEnabled && interleavedProgress === true && hasReasoningLane` — inherits every existing group/DM/visibility gate.

## What it does

When `channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress` is enabled:
- Reasoning text streams into the interleaved message with a "Thinking" header
- Tool starts append a sanitized line (tool name only, never raw args by default)
- Item/plan/approval/command/patch events append title-only summary lines
- A rolling timer (20s cadence) shows elapsed time + a wall-clock stamp while running, so a stalled lane is visible at a glance; cleared on completion
- The model's reply is delivered once as the polished answer message — it is **not** mixed into the Thinking lane
- Long turns spill into continuation messages instead of freezing at Telegram's length cap
- Falls back to the existing `pushStreamToolProgress` lane when the flag is off

## Key design choices

- **Projection-only** — no changes to prompts, model routing, tool execution, or auth
- **No raw data** — tool lines show name only; `interleavedToolArgs` is a separate opt-in for sanitized args
- **No new event contract** — consumes existing `onReasoningStream`, `onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary` callbacks unchanged
- **Reasoning + tools only; reply stays out** — the lane renders reasoning (`onReasoningStream`) and tool/event lines; the model's reply prose is **not** folded into it. Folding reply text in and stripping it back out at delivery proved unreliable — the model often drafts an answer, runs tools, then re-emits a revised one, leaving several answer-like copies of which only the last matches the polished final text. So the reply is delivered exactly once, as the answer. For the interactive backend the proxy restores real thinking on its own `onReasoningStream`, so reasoning is unaffected; a defensive final-answer strip remains for redacted-thinking backends that bridge assistant text into the reasoning lane. Tagged payloads (`<think>reason</think>answer`) keep only the in-tag reasoning, so answer prose after `</think>` never lands in the lane.
- **Single reasoning stream, delta-append** — appended as monotonic deltas (a cumulative snapshot contributes only its new suffix), so re-delivery never duplicates and the lane reads chronologically reasoning → tool → reasoning → …. The overlap fold is guarded: it folds a full-tail re-send (and full-increment duplicates) but never a short/mid-word coincidence, so a fresh fragment never loses its leading character.
- **Continuation on overflow** — as the durable message approaches Telegram's 4096-char cap the lane spills into a fresh continuation message (carrying recent context) instead of letting the draft stream stop editing and freeze the lane
- **Rate-limit-aware cadence** — the rolling "still running" timer ticks every 20s (not every few seconds) so N concurrently-streaming Thinking messages stay under Telegram's per-chat edit rate and don't draw 429s (a 429 drops the edit and stalls the lane); the line carries a wall-clock stamp so a genuine stall is obvious
- **No empty Thinking** — a turn that reaches final delivery with no reasoning/tool/status content (and no running timer) renders nothing, so the lane never emits a bare "Thinking" header
- **Plain-text transcript** — reasoning tag markers (`<think>` etc.) are unwrapped so the lane shows the model's prose, never raw tags

## Prerequisite

- **#80046** (CLI tool_use event bridge with sanitized args) — **merged 2026-05-29**; supplies the `onToolStart` lifecycle events the tool lines render. This branch should be rebased onto current `main` (which now contains it) before merge.

The renderer otherwise consumes only existing callbacks and is default-off, so it depends on no unmerged work. (On the standard `claude-cli` backend, CLI-*native* reasoning text additionally benefits from #85381's `thinking_delta`; without it the lane simply shows tool/event lines. The interactive backend restores thinking via its proxy, so reasoning appears regardless. Either way the default tool-progress lane is unaffected — this is not a hard dependency.)

## Changed files

- `extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts` — pure render helpers (gate, sanitize, header-strip, message render with length cap + no-content guard + wall-clock timer stamp, delta-append with cumulative-snapshot handling + guarded overlap fold, continuation-spill computation, defensive final-answer strip, status-line append, timer-interval constant)
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` — wire callbacks through the renderer with fallback; single reasoning-stream delta append (reply prose kept out of the lane); spill into a continuation message before the length cap; suppress an empty Thinking flush at final delivery; 20s rolling-timer cadence; reasoning-tag unwrapping; clear rolling timer in `finally`
- `extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts` — `stripReasoningTagsForInterleaved` (extract only in-`<think>` reasoning, dropping answer prose after `</think>`, preserving code regions)
- `src/config/types.telegram.ts` + `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` — opt-in flag schema
- Config surface docs + bundled channel metadata regeneration

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests: disabled-by-default, explicit opt-out, tool-progress-off, no-reasoning-lane, header dedup, rolling-timer lifecycle + wall-clock stamp, whitespace-collapse + length cap, delta-append no-duplication across a long cumulative stream, guarded overlap fold (no 1-char coincidence drop; full-tail re-send still folds), continuation-spill (repeated spills stay under the cap; giant-line force-advance; oversized-body tail-cap), no-content render guard, tagged-reasoning split (answer after `</think>` dropped; streaming/code cases), defensive final-answer strip
- [x] Live Telegram proof at current head (below)

## Verification

Local checks at the current head:

```
pnpm tsgo:extensions                                                 # typecheck — clean
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts \
                 extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts \
                 extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts   # lint — clean
npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.test.ts \
               extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.test.ts   # all green
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Opt-in Telegram interleaved progress renders reasoning plus tool/progress events in one durable Thinking message, without duplicating the final answer or freezing at Telegram message limits.

Real environment tested: Real OpenClaw Telegram gateway against Telegram, default-off feature explicitly enabled, two concurrent Telegram threads in one chat.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rebuilt and ran the gateway from the PR head, enabled channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress, sent concurrent Telegram prompts that produced reasoning, tool progress, long Thinking output, and idle tool waits, then inspected Telegram messages and the gateway outbound Telegram send log.

Evidence after fix: Screenshot embedded below plus redacted outbound Telegram send-log metrics copied below: continuation msg=34783 -> msg=34786, final answer absent from every Thinking edit, zero bare Thinking sends, 20s timer edits at 04:20:15 -> 04:20:35 -> 04:20:55, and 99 outbound calls with 0 failures.

Observed result after fix: Thinking lane showed reasoning plus tool line, rolled into a continuation before the cap, sent the polished answer once as its own message, cleared empty Thinking output, and avoided Telegram 429s under the two-thread concurrent run.

What was not tested: No broad channel matrix or release package lane in this PR; focused local proof covers the Telegram renderer helpers, schema, lint, and extension typecheck.

## Live proof (current head)

Captured on the current head with the default-off feature enabled, over a **two-thread concurrent** session. The screenshot shows the visible lane; the rest is verified from the gateway's **outbound Telegram send log** — every `sendMessage` / `editMessageText` the bot issues — which shows message-level behavior a screenshot can't (message ids, edit cadence, final-state content, timing). Wire-level facts below are message-level metrics (ids, lengths, ok/timing) and contain no command/path/host content.

**Normal interleaving — reasoning + tool-use line in the Thinking lane, with the rolling timer:**

![interleaved lane](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/66da4d16b5a9467a82a65134976bc66aa956519c/proof-assets/interleaved-progress-telegram-2026-05-30-toolline.png)

**Wire-level verification (gateway send log, current head, one two-thread concurrent session):**

- **Long-message continuation** — `msg=34783` grew to the 4000-char cap and rolled into a fresh continuation `msg=34786` (carrying the next tool line) instead of freezing; clean seam, no garble.
- **No duplicate final answer** — on that long thinking-heavy turn the final answer was delivered once as its own message and is **absent from every Thinking edit** of `34783`/`34786`.
- **No empty Thinking** — **zero** bare-`Thinking`-header sends across the session.
- **20s rolling-timer cadence (rate-limit relief)** — while idling on a blocking tool, the "still running" line re-edited at **exactly 20s intervals** (04:20:15 → 04:20:35 → 04:20:55, body static at 3764 chars), keeping concurrent Thinking messages under Telegram's edit rate. The wall-clock stamp exposes any genuine stall.
- **No rate-limiting** — **99** outbound calls across the two concurrent threads, **0** failures (no `429`s, no oversized-preview stop) — the exact concurrency that previously drew 429s.

target.title: fix(doctor): respect explicit PI runtime policy
target.body:
## Summary

Respect explicit PI/OpenClaw runtime policy when Codex is intentionally unavailable.

This keeps the existing OpenAI/Codex default behavior unchanged, but makes explicit PI config authoritative in three places:

- config validation no longer emits a missing `@openclaw/codex` install warning when OpenAI routes are explicitly pinned to PI/OpenClaw or `plugins.entries.codex.enabled` is explicitly `false`
- doctor stale-plugin diagnostics suppress only intentional `plugins.entries.codex` missing-plugin noise while still reporting legitimate stale policy refs (`plugins.allow` / `plugins.deny`) and explicitly enabled missing Codex entries
- doctor session route repair preserves explicit PI/OpenClaw session runtime pins while rewriting stale `openai-codex/*` route metadata to canonical `openai/*`

The missing-Codex suppression intentionally does **not** apply when `plugins.entries.codex.enabled === true`, even if OpenAI is pinned to PI/OpenClaw; that remains a real missing-plugin warning.

## Tests

- `npm exec -- pnpm test -- --run src/config/config.plugin-validation.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/stale-plugin-config.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts`
  - config plugin validation: 43 passed
  - stale-plugin config: 15 passed
  - Codex route warnings: 105 passed
- `npm exec -- pnpm check:test-types`
- `npm exec -- pnpm lint:core`

## Real behavior proof

Commands were run from this branch after rebuilding source checkout dist via `pnpm dev`. Paths below use isolated temp homes; no real user config was modified.

### Explicit PI/OpenClaw policy + missing Codex entry: quiet

Config excerpt:

```json
{
  "models": {
    "providers": {
      "openai": {
        "baseUrl": "https://api.openai.com/v1",
        "models": [],
        "agentRuntime": { "id": "pi" }
      }
    }
  },
  "plugins": { "entries": { "codex": {} } },
  "agents": { "list": [{ "id": "openclaw" }] }
}
```

Command:

```sh
OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 \
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/oc-proof-pr88723-disabled-bundled/home \
HOME=/tmp/oc-proof-pr88723-disabled-bundled/home \
npm exec -- pnpm -- dev doctor
```

Codex/stale diagnostic grep:

```text
--- codex/stale/plugin installed lines ---
│  - model-usage: bins: codexbar                                           │
│    install option: Install CodexBar (brew cask)                          │
```

No `plugins.entries.codex`, `plugin not installed: codex`, or stale `codex` plugin diagnostic is emitted for the intentional PI/OpenClaw setup.

### Explicitly enabled missing Codex plugin: still warns

Config is the same except:

```json
"plugins": { "entries": { "codex": { "enabled": true } } }
```

Command:

```sh
OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 \
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/oc-proof-pr88723-enabled-bundled/home \
HOME=/tmp/oc-proof-pr88723-enabled-bundled/home \
npm exec -- pnpm -- dev doctor
```

Relevant doctor output:

```text
◇  Config warnings ────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│  - plugins.entries.codex: plugin not installed: codex — install the  │
│    official external plugin with: openclaw plugins install           │
│    @openclaw/codex                                                   │

◇  Doctor warnings ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│  - plugins.entries.codex: stale plugin reference "codex" was found.     │
│  - Run "openclaw doctor --fix" to remove stale plugin ids and dangling  │
│    channel references.                                                  │
```

Refs #88706.

target.title: fix(agents): expose session status route context
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `session_status` now exposes route metadata that separates where a session originated from where the current live run is active.
- A Discord-origin session continued from webchat can now show `origin`, `active`, and persisted `deliveryContext` without requiring agents to infer meaning from the session key string.
- The route context is included both in structured `details` and in the visible tool text because tool-result details are not replayed back into model context.

Why does this matter now?

- #84544 reports agents confusing the origin-channel session key with the current channel after a cross-surface continuation.
- `sessions_list` already exposes route metadata, but `session_status` was still ambiguous for the common `sessionKey: "current"` check.

What is the intended outcome?

- Agents can tell that `agent:main:discord:channel:...` is the origin/persisted delivery route while the active run is currently on webchat.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- This does not change session-key generation or delivery routing.
- This does not add new config, migrations, or channel-specific policy.
- This does not change model/status-card semantics outside appending route context.

What does success look like?

- `session_status({ sessionKey: "current" })` returns a visible `Route context` JSON block plus structured `origin`, `active`, and `deliveryContext` fields when route metadata exists.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether `active` is only reported for the live run session, while explicit non-live lookups keep only origin/persisted delivery context.
- Whether the nested field names align with the existing `sessions_list` route metadata rather than adding parallel aliases.

AI-assisted; contributor manually reviewed and is responsible for the change.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #84544

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #88316

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request; this follows the open bug report.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `session_status` lacked machine-readable and model-visible route metadata, so a session key with a Discord-origin prefix could be mistaken for the current live channel after continuation from webchat.
- Real environment tested: Local source checkout on this branch, using the production `createOpenClawTools(...)` factory and production `session_status` tool with a temporary session store and temporary OpenClaw home/state.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_HOME=$(mktemp -d /tmp/openclaw-84544-proof-home-XXXXXX) pnpm exec tsx /tmp/openclaw-84544-red-repro.ts
```

- Evidence after fix:

Terminal capture from this branch, copied live output excerpt:

```json
{
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:discord:channel:1489550370136129537",
  "origin": {
    "provider": "discord",
    "accountId": "guild-1"
  },
  "active": {
    "channel": "webchat",
    "to": "webchat:control-ui",
    "threadId": "webchat-thread-1"
  },
  "deliveryContext": {
    "channel": "discord",
    "to": "channel:1489550370136129537",
    "accountId": "guild-1",
    "threadId": "thread-1"
  }
}
```

The same run also printed a visible `Route context:` JSON block in the tool text.

- Observed result after fix: The semantic current lookup resolves to the Discord-origin session key while exposing `origin.provider = "discord"`, `active.channel = "webchat"`, and persisted `deliveryContext.channel = "discord"`. The script exits successfully only after asserting all three fields.
- What was not tested: No live Discord or webchat service was contacted; the proof uses production source code with a synthetic temporary session store.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This validates the tool contract and factory wiring locally. It does not prove external transport delivery.
- Before evidence: The same script failed on current `main` because `details.origin` was absent; current output only contained `ok`, `sessionKey`, `changedModel`, and `statusText`.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.tts-config.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo
OPENCLAW_HOME=$(mktemp -d /tmp/openclaw-84544-proof-home-XXXXXX) pnpm exec tsx /tmp/openclaw-84544-red-repro.ts
git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added `session_status` coverage for semantic-current route metadata (`origin`, `active`, `deliveryContext`).
- Added coverage that explicit non-live lookups do not inherit the current active route.
- Added `createOpenClawTools` wiring coverage for passing the active live-run route into `session_status`.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The synthetic production-source repro failed on current `main` with missing `origin` route metadata.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. `session_status` text can now include an additional `Route context` JSON block when route metadata exists.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

The tool output contract: route metadata must not imply the persisted origin channel is the current live route.

How is that risk mitigated?

The patch keeps nested fields separate, reports `active` only for the live run session, and covers both semantic-current and explicit non-live lookups.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review after branch publication.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and maintainer review after publication.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

None yet on this PR.

target.title: fix(agents): wait for cron media completions
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep cron media generation detached, but suppress the interactive yield path and make the embedded runner wait for cron-owned image, music, and video tasks to finish before final closeout.
- Record async task run IDs from tool results, with a task-registry fallback for already-active run-scoped media tasks and timeout-aware completion handling.
- Relax generated-media delivery only for no-target cron completion handoffs, where there is no external reply target to satisfy.

Fixes #88001.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.async-tasks.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.tts-config.test.ts` (7 unit-fast tests, 157 agent tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-trajectory-status.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.session-identity.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts` (16 unit-fast tests, 49 cron tests, 253 agent tests)
- `git diff --check`

target.title: feat: add Agent OS full-local substrate
target.body:
## Summary

Adds the first Agent OS alpha substrate as a local-first, proof-native runtime layer:

- Defines native Agent OS contracts for tickets, capability manifests, proof events, artifacts, lifecycle/status, and sandbox/security docs.
- Adds full-local sidecars for Blackboard dispatch, signal-hub routing, proof journaling, Sentinel model proxying, memory wiki readiness, and Windows-native bridge dispatch.
- Packages capability agent scripts, proof kit, sidecar scripts, and docs so a tarball install includes the runnable substrate.
- Hardens default local exposure: Docker-published Gateway, bridge, Teams, and Sentinel ports default to `127.0.0.1`; Sentinel requires a token by default; sidecars run with dropped network caps and `no-new-privileges`.
- Adds restart-safe Blackboard/proof DB behavior with busy timeouts, host checkpointing, and durable journal-mode allowlisting.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: OpenClaw can boot a full-local Agent OS alpha stack, accept a Blackboard task, route it through a capability agent path, produce proof/artifacts, restart signal-hub, and prove ticket/proof state survives restart while keeping host-published ports loopback-only by default.
- Real environment tested: Windows host with Docker Desktop, rebuilt `openclaw:local` image from this branch, full-local compose stack on alternate host ports `18799`, `18800`, `3979`, and `18899`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: rebuilt with `node scripts/docker/full-local.mjs up`, then ran `node scripts/docker/full-local.mjs status`, `node scripts/docker/full-local.mjs smoke`, `node scripts/docker/full-local.mjs golden`, and checked Docker-published ports with `docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.sidecars.yml --profile local-sidecars port ...` using `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=18799 OPENCLAW_SENTINEL_PORT=18899 OPENCLAW_BRIDGE_PORT=18800 OPENCLAW_MSTEAMS_PORT=3979`.
- Evidence after fix: copied live output from the rebuilt Docker stack showed `Full local proof: ready`, `OK service:openclaw-gateway`, `OK service:openclaw-sentinel`, `OK service:openclaw-signal-hub`, `OK service:openclaw-obsidian-syncer`, `OK gateway:readyz`, `OK sentinel:readyz`, `OK memory-wiki:status`; Docker port output showed `127.0.0.1:18799`, `127.0.0.1:18800`, `127.0.0.1:3979`, and `127.0.0.1:18899`; smoke produced ticket `a1fc09b3-5469-4603-8bcd-7c7d9bb2d21d` with status `DONE`; the golden artifact reported ticket `591c3f07-8c31-474d-9ced-9cd80987e512`, `ok: true`, `failedChecks: []`, `restartJournalMode: DELETE`, and `readinessAfterRestart: true`.
- Observed result after fix: The rebuilt full-local stack stayed healthy after restart proof, delivered the smoke ticket, kept all host-published ports on loopback, and generated a golden E2E proof bundle with no failed checks.
- What was not tested: Remote Crabbox/Testbox proof is still blocked from this machine because `crabbox` failed wrapper sanity checks and `blacksmith` is not installed; this draft PR needs maintainer CI/Testbox validation for remote proof.

## Additional verification

```sh

```

- Sentinel gateway tests: `30 passed`.

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 10.33.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `25243d99b2009a0c437874ed68dca24ad8c8614c`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `pnpm openclaw agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-25243d99b2` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof.NRgAFc/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof.NRgAFc/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof pnpm openclaw agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-25243d99b2 --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the CLI run and request-log assertion:

```text
[agent/embedded] [trace:embedded-run] startup stages: runId=c43c486b-23d0-4e83-8fd1-c0dd12f67cba sessionId=proof-subagent-session phase=attempt-dispatch totalMs=5818 stages=workspace:1ms@1ms,runtime-plugins:5605ms@5606ms,hooks:6ms@5612ms,model-resolution:113ms@5725ms,auth:67ms@5792ms,context-engine:1ms@5793ms,attempt-workspace:1ms@5794ms,attempt-prompt:0ms@5794ms,attempt-runtime-plan:24ms@5818ms,attempt-dispatch:0ms@5818ms
```

```json
{
  "head": "25243d99b2009a0c437874ed68dca24ad8c8614c",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-25243d99b2",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": ["exec", "read"],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported runtimes, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, and appears in model-facing dynamic tool snapshots.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native embedded subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. Unsupported ACP and CLI-backed native paths reject the option clearly.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native embedded subagent runs. Unsupported paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced.

## Repro + Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 5 files, 159 tests, 3 Vitest shards.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...changed files...` passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` passed: `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=dot src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` passed locally after CI showed a timeout/interrupt flake in that unrelated file: 1 file, 48 tests.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed before the snapshot commit; `pnpm openclaw agent --help` rebuilt dist successfully after the snapshot commit because the CLI detected a new git head.
- Current-head CLI/mock OpenAI proof above passed.

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: schema exposure, native forwarding path, Gateway forwarding path, session persistence, embedded command runner propagation, persisted child-session allowlist inheritance, strict validation tests, ACP rejection tests, CLI-backed native fail-closed behavior for non-empty and empty allowlists, embedded empty-allowlist behavior, prompt snapshot update, formatter/typecheck/build/whitespace checks.
- Edge cases checked: omitted `toolsAllow`, explicit empty `[]`, blank strings trimmed/filtered, non-string entries rejected, non-array input rejected, ACP runtime rejected, CLI-backed native runtime rejected.
- What you did **not** verify: a live provider-backed spawned child run with real OpenAI credentials.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect CLI-backed native runs.
  - Mitigation: CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch because they cannot enforce the embedded-runner allowlist.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses the existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.title: fix(ci): guard workflow template injection
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: #68428 tracks `zizmor` template-injection findings in GitHub workflow shell blocks; latest `main` had the originally listed workflows fixed, but an all-workflow scan still found the same class in `windows-testbox-probe.yml`.
- Why it matters: workflow_dispatch inputs can be attacker-controlled enough for `zizmor` to flag direct shell interpolation, and only checking changed workflows can miss stale findings in untouched workflow files.
- What changed: moved the Windows probe `target_ref` expression into step `env`, added a reasoned `secrets-inherit` suppression for the maintainer-only Update Migration package-acceptance lane, and made `check:workflows` / Workflow Sanity run the pinned `zizmor` hook across all workflow YAML files.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no product runtime behavior, release package behavior, or workflow permissions were expanded.

target.title: feat(context-engine): pass runtime settings into lifecycle
target.body:
## Summary

Adds an OpenClaw-owned `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` contract and passes it into context-engine lifecycle calls.

This is intentionally framed as a general OpenClaw context-engine programming contract, not a HyperMem-specific shim. Core now builds a typed runtime settings object from the active host/runtime/model context and supplies it to lifecycle hooks so context engines do not need to infer runtime facts from globals, logs, provider strings, or side channels.

What changed:

- Adds exported `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` plus typed unavailable/unsupported runtime-settings errors.
- Adds `buildContextEngineRuntimeSettings()` in `src/context-engine/runtime-settings.ts`.
- Passes `runtimeSettings` into `bootstrap`, `assemble`, `afterTurn`, and `maintain` params.
- Wires runtime settings through the shared harness lifecycle, Codex app-server attempt path, embedded runner attempt path, generic CLI runner path, and deferred context-engine maintenance.
- Keeps lifecycle param additions optional for legacy context-engine compatibility.

## Validation

```text
git diff --check
```

Observed result on `9d54285b0d`:

After rebasing cleanly onto current `origin/main` at `6653193fdb`:


Observed result after rebase:

  - `src/gateway/server-tailscale.test.ts(108,48): Argument of type '"node.tailnet.ts.net"' is not assignable to parameter of type 'null'.`
  - `src/gateway/server-tailscale.test.ts(130,48): Argument of type '"node.tailnet.ts.net"' is not assignable to parameter of type 'null'.`

  - `src/gateway/server-tailscale.test.ts(169,48): Argument of type '"node"' is not assignable to parameter of type 'null'.`

target.body:
## Summary

- Allow the internal `webchat` channel section in config validation.
- Add a strict WebChat channel schema for `textChunkLimit`, `chunkMode`, `streaming.chunkMode`, and per-account overrides.
- Cover the reported config path with a regression test while keeping unknown channel typos fatal.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `channels.webchat` with `textChunkLimit` and `chunkMode` is accepted by config validation instead of failing as an unknown channel id.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source CLI in an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`, with bundled plugins disabled to keep the proof focused on config validation.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `HOME=$EVIDENCE_DIR/webchat-config-source-home OPENCLAW_HOME=$EVIDENCE_DIR/webchat-config-source-home OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=$EVIDENCE_DIR/webchat-config-source-home/.openclaw OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 pnpm -C $TASK_WORKTREE exec node --import tsx src/entry.ts config validate`
- **Evidence after fix:** `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-87897-evidence/webchat-config-source-validate-review.txt`
- **Observed result after fix:** `Config valid: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json`
- **What was not tested:** Full browser Web UI rendering and gateway startup were not exercised; this patch targets the config validation boundary and the existing WebChat chunk-limit runtime resolver.
- **Target test file:** `src/config/config.plugin-validation.test.ts`

## Regression Test Plan

- `pnpm -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-87897 exec vitest run src/config/config.plugin-validation.test.ts -t "WebChat text chunk"`
- `pnpm -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-87897 exec vitest run src/config/config.plugin-validation.test.ts`
- `pnpm -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-87897 exec vitest run src/auto-reply/chunk.test.ts -t "resolveTextChunkLimit|resolveChunkMode"`
- `daily_fix.sh prepush`

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The WebChat chunk resolver already reads `channels.webchat`, but the channel validation allowlist only included top-level defaults/model overrides and bundled or plugin channel ids. Because WebChat is an internal surface rather than a bundled channel plugin, `channels.webchat` was rejected before the chunk settings could be used.

target.title: fix(codex): avoid guardian review for local models
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Prevents Codex app-server Guardian / `auto_review` from being used as the approval reviewer when the active model provider is local or custom, such as LM Studio.
- Keeps Codex/OpenAI app-server runs on the native model-backed approval reviewer path.
- Routes local/custom Codex app-server command approvals through OpenClaw's own `tools.exec.mode=auto` reviewer when the app-server reviewer has been downgraded to `user`.
- Applies the same provider-aware reviewer policy to normal Codex app-server runs, bound conversation turns, native code-mode-only request shaping, and side-question forks.

Why does this matter now?

- `tools.exec.mode=auto` forced Codex Guardian before checking the selected model provider. A local model routed through the Codex harness could therefore be treated as if Codex Guardian could safely review it.
- If the user configured an OpenAI reviewer model for exec auto-review while running a local/custom main model, the app-server path previously had no internal OpenClaw reviewer bridge after Guardian was disabled.

What is the intended outcome?

- Codex-native and OpenAI-backed Codex app-server runs can keep native `auto_review`.
- Local/custom providers fall back to `approvalsReviewer: "user"` while retaining `approvalPolicy: "on-request"` and workspace sandboxing.
- When `tools.exec.mode=auto` is active, command approvals can be reviewed by OpenClaw's configured exec reviewer model through the internal simple-completion harness, including the existing default behavior where omitted `tools.exec.reviewer.model` reuses the current agent model.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to Codex app-server protocol fields or provider catalog configuration.
- No live local-model provider round trip in this PR.

What does success look like?

- `lmstudio` / local provider Codex runs no longer send `approvalsReviewer: "auto_review"` or `guardian_subagent`.
- Existing provider-qualified legacy bindings such as `lmstudio/local-model` are handled safely even without a stored `modelProvider`.
- Local/custom app-server command approvals in exec auto mode either use OpenClaw's internal reviewer or fall back to the existing plugin/human approval route when the request is not safe for model auto-review.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The provider gate in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.ts`.
- The app-server approval bridge in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.ts`.
- The SDK helper in `src/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime.ts` that builds parser-bound exec auto-review input.
- Side-question fork policy resolution in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.ts`.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Maintainer-requested investigation and fix during local debugging.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: local/custom Codex harness model providers no longer use Codex Guardian / native model-backed approval review for exec auto mode; app-server command approvals can instead use OpenClaw's internal exec reviewer model path when safe.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw worktree on the Codex plugin test harness, plus Blacksmith Testbox `tbx_01kszp8dzjvt2r50r1yw5gdp40` against pushed head `448efb65b377eafb6440ebf1ae1af0e25deab8c1`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/infra/exec-auto-review.ts src/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.ts`; `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/exec-auto-review.ts src/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.ts`; `git diff --check`; `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts src/agents/exec-auto-reviewer.test.ts src/infra/exec-auto-review.test.ts`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --shell -- "set -euo pipefail; env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=true corepack pnpm check:changed"`.
- Evidence after fix: local focused Vitest reported 7 passed files / 204 passed tests; local oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, and plugin SDK API baseline check passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings; Testbox `check:changed` passed on `tbx_01kszp8dzjvt2r50r1yw5gdp40`.
- Observed result after fix: mocked `lmstudio` Codex app-server start/turn, code-mode-only, side-question, and bound-conversation flows keep prompting approval and sandboxing but send `approvalsReviewer: "user"`; Codex/OpenAI providers still keep `auto_review`; app-server auto command approvals call OpenClaw's exec reviewer only for parser-bound single commands and skip to plugin approval for amendments, restricted approval choices, security-audit suppression edits, unbound shell chains, reviewer `ask`, or run cancellation.
- What was not tested: a live LM Studio or Ollama app-server round trip with a real model process.

The PR-touched files and changed-gate proof pass.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): existing config resolution forced `tools.exec.mode=auto` into Codex app-server `approvalsReviewer: "auto_review"` before considering the selected model provider, and app-server approval fallback did not consult OpenClaw's configured exec reviewer model.

## Tests and validation

- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/infra/exec-auto-review.ts src/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.ts`

## Summary
- Add a key-free FxTwitter exact-post reader for public X/Twitter status URLs and numeric status IDs.
- Route exact post references through FxTwitter before falling back to xAI-backed broader `x_search`.
- Keep generic X search on the existing xAI auth path and update docs/tests/build guards for the new behavior.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** OpenClaw can read an exact public X/Twitter status URL through `x_search` without requiring an xAI API key for that exact post read.
- **Real environment tested:** Local macOS OpenClaw Gateway/Dashboard, built from this patch, restarted under LaunchAgent, listening on `127.0.0.1:18789` with the promoted runtime snapshot.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Built OpenClaw, restarted the Gateway, verified `openclaw gateway status --deep`, then invoked the live Gateway HTTP tool endpoint with `curl` against `x_search` using `https://x.com/jack/status/20`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the real Gateway invocation:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "provider": "fxtwitter",
  "mode": "exact_post",
  "statusId": "20",
  "hasExpectedText": true
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The live Gateway returned `provider=fxtwitter`, `mode=exact_post`, `statusId=20`, and confirmed the expected post text was present (`just setting up my twttr`). This proves the exact-post path used FxTwitter and worked on a real running OpenClaw setup.
- **What was not tested:** No known gaps for the exact public post-read path. Broader xAI-backed generic X search remains covered by targeted tests and still requires xAI auth.

target.title: fix(openai): avoid stale Responses message id replay
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenAI Responses request replay can reuse a signed assistant `message` item id after the corresponding `reasoning` item has been pruned from local history.
- Solution: replay signed assistant message ids only when the immediately preceding replayed item is the paired reasoning item.
- What changed: added a small replay-id policy helper, wired it into the OpenAI Responses transport, and covered the pruned-reasoning case in the existing reasoning replay tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not change reasoning-item replay, tool-call replay, payload metadata, model selection, or Telegram formatting.

## Motivation

This prevents a latent OpenAI Responses failure exposed by long-running sessions whose assistant text still has a persisted Responses item id but whose paired reasoning item is no longer replayable. In that state, the next request can be rejected before the model responds.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenAI Responses rejected replayed conversation history when an assistant `message` item id was included without the required paired `reasoning` item, while phase metadata still needs to survive without replaying orphan provider-owned assistant ids.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS arm64 using the OpenAI Responses transport path, plus a live OpenAI Responses API request against `gpt-5.5` on May 31, 2026 after maintainer fixup `d6902ed1a0f13c7b73e69fcfdc6566086b3c532f`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```shell
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'INNER'
import OpenAI from 'openai';
import { buildOpenAIResponsesParams } from './src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts';

const model = {
  id: 'gpt-5.4',
  name: 'gpt-5.4',
  api: 'openai-responses',
  provider: 'openai',
  baseUrl: 'https://api.openai.com/v1',
  reasoning: true,
  input: ['text'],
  cost: { input: 0, output: 0, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0 },
  contextWindow: 128000,
  maxTokens: 4096,
};
const ZERO_USAGE = {
  input: 0,
  output: 0,
  cacheRead: 0,
  cacheWrite: 0,
  totalTokens: 0,
  cost: { input: 0, output: 0, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0, total: 0 },
};
const context = {
  systemPrompt: 'system',
  messages: [
    { role: 'user', content: 'Earlier prompt', timestamp: 1 },
    {
      role: 'assistant',
      api: 'openai-responses',
      provider: 'openai',
      model: 'gpt-5.4',
      usage: ZERO_USAGE,
      stopReason: 'stop',
      timestamp: 2,
      content: [
        {
          type: 'text',
          text: 'Earlier answer',
          textSignature: 'msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning',
        },
      ],
    },
    {
      role: 'assistant',
      api: 'openai-responses',
      provider: 'openai',
      model: 'gpt-5.4',
      usage: ZERO_USAGE,
      stopReason: 'stop',
      timestamp: 2,
      content: [
        {
          type: 'thinking',
          thinking: 'chain',
          thinkingSignature: JSON.stringify({
            id: 'rs_reasoning_item',
            type: 'reasoning',
            summary: [],
          }),
        },
        {
          type: 'text',
          text: 'Pinned commentary answer',
          textSignature: JSON.stringify({ v: 1, id: 'msg_commentary', phase: 'commentary' }),
        },
      ],
    },
    { role: 'user', content: 'Latest prompt', timestamp: 3 },
  ],
};
const params = buildOpenAIResponsesParams(model, context, {});
const captured = {};
const client = new OpenAI({
  apiKey: 'test',
  baseURL: 'https://api.openai.com/v1',
  fetch: async (url, init) => {
    captured.url = String(url);
    captured.body = init?.body ? String(init.body) : '';
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({
      id: 'resp_test',
      object: 'response',
      created_at: 0,
      status: 'completed',
      model: 'gpt-5.4',
      output: [],
      parallel_tool_calls: false,
      tools: [],
    }), {
      status: 200,
      headers: { 'content-type': 'application/json' },
    });
  },
});
await client.responses.create({ ...params, stream: false });
const payload = JSON.stringify(params.input);
console.log(JSON.stringify({
  proof: 'openai-responses-sdk-loopback',
  pr_head: 'b60fd8baa5901ec405764edfa476b867f65c1f60',
  request_path: new URL(captured.url).pathname,
  stale_signed_message_id: 'msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning',
  stale_id_present_in_openclaw_payload: payload.includes('msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning'),
  stale_id_present_in_sdk_request: captured.body.includes('msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning'),
  commentary_phase_id_present_in_openclaw_payload: payload.includes('msg_commentary'),
  commentary_phase_id_present_in_sdk_request: captured.body.includes('msg_commentary'),
}, null, 2));
INNER
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

Before this patch, the live OpenClaw Telegram agent diagnosed a real OpenAI Responses failure after conversation pruning:

```text
OpenAI rejected the next request with:
message was provided without its required reasoning item

Failure shape:
- prior assistant message ids were replayed from textSignature
- paired reasoning items had been pruned/unavailable
```

After this patch, I reran the current-head OpenAI SDK loopback on `b60fd8baa5901ec405764edfa476b867f65c1f60`. It imported `buildOpenAIResponsesParams` from `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`, sent that exact payload through OpenAI Node SDK 6.39.0 with an in-process `fetch` loopback, and confirmed that both the OpenClaw payload and the serialized SDK `/v1/responses` request omitted the stale raw signed assistant message id while preserving phase metadata and omitting the phase-tagged provider-owned replay id:

```json
{
  "proof": "openai-responses-sdk-loopback",
  "pr_head": "b60fd8baa5901ec405764edfa476b867f65c1f60",
  "request_path": "/v1/responses",
  "stale_signed_message_id": "msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning",
  "stale_id_present_in_openclaw_payload": false,
  "stale_id_present_in_sdk_request": false,
  "commentary_phase_id_present_in_openclaw_payload": true,
  "commentary_phase_id_present_in_sdk_request": true
}
```

Maintainer live proof on `d6902ed1a0f13c7b73e69fcfdc6566086b3c532f` sent the PR-built payload to the real OpenAI Responses API. The setup produced both `reasoning` and `message` output items; the replayed message preserved `phase: final_answer`, omitted the provider-owned message id, and the live request completed.

```json
{
  "proof": "live-openai-responses-phase-id-omitted",
  "model": "gpt-5.5",
  "setup_output_types": ["reasoning", "message"],
  "replayed_message_has_id": false,
  "replayed_message_phase": "final_answer",
  "live_result_status": "completed"
}
```

- Observed result after fix: stale raw signed assistant message ids are omitted when their required reasoning item is absent; phase-tagged provider ids are omitted without losing phase metadata, and unsigned assistant messages still get the existing synthetic fallback id.
- What was not tested: a live deployed gateway restart has not been run; focused replay tests now pass via `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/openai-responses.reasoning-replay.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts` (3 shards, 228 tests), alongside a live OpenAI Responses API proof.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): live OpenClaw agent output above captured the OpenAI rejection text and identified the pruned-reasoning replay shape.


## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the Responses replay path treated persisted assistant text signatures as independently replayable message ids, but OpenAI can require the corresponding reasoning item to be replayed with that message item.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing coverage checked reasoning replay when reasoning was present; it did not check the pruned-reasoning case where only the signed assistant message survived.
- Contributing context (if known): long-lived sessions can prune or omit reasoning items while retaining assistant text signatures.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/openai-responses.reasoning-replay.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: a signed assistant message id is omitted when no paired reasoning item was replayed; the same signed id is preserved when paired reasoning was replayed; unsigned text still uses the fallback id.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in request payload construction, before network IO, so the replay-id policy can be tested deterministically.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none for the pruned-reasoning signed-message case.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None, except affected OpenAI Responses sessions avoid a provider-side 400 when replay history has a stale signed message id without its paired reasoning item.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[assistant textSignature id survives] -> [reasoning item pruned] -> [request replays message id] -> [OpenAI rejects]

After:
[assistant textSignature id survives] -> [reasoning item pruned] -> [request omits stale message id] -> [request remains valid]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS arm64
- Runtime/container: local Node/Vitest via OpenClaw source checkout
- Model/provider: `openai/gpt-5.4`, OpenAI Responses
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram agent session exposed the failure; source regression covers the transport replay policy.
- Relevant config (redacted): OpenAI Responses API model with reasoning enabled.

### Steps

1. Create a replay candidate with a signed assistant text `message` id.
2. Omit the paired reasoning item, matching a pruned history state.
3. Build the next OpenAI Responses request.

### Expected

- The stale signed assistant message id is omitted when paired reasoning is unavailable.

### Actual

- Before this change, the stale signed assistant message id was replayed even when the paired reasoning item was unavailable.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: live OpenAI Responses `gpt-5.5` API proof via an inline `node --import tsx --input-type=module` script that imported `buildOpenAIResponsesParams` from `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`; focused replay regression coverage via `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/openai-responses.reasoning-replay.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts` (3 shards, 228 tests); `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on touched files; `git diff --check`; autoreview clean.
- Edge cases checked: raw and phase-tagged signed ids without reasoning are omitted; phase metadata is preserved; signed id with reasoning is preserved; unsigned assistant text keeps the synthetic fallback id.
- What you did **not** verify: live gateway deployment on this branch.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: omitting a stale Responses message id may reduce provider-side continuity for that one historical assistant message.
  - Mitigation: the text content and phase metadata are still replayed; the id is omitted only when the paired reasoning item is missing, which is the provider-rejected shape.

target.title: feat(hooks): add agent:turn:end internal hook event
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: ACP turn completion updates latency/error stats, but there was no internal hook that fires once a turn has fully completed, so post-turn automation had no single reliable end-of-turn seam.
- Why it matters: Existing signals are per-message, sub-turn, or session-level, which makes analytics and post-turn workflows awkward and can race transcript persistence.
- What changed: Added a narrow `agent:turn:end` internal hook emitted from `recordTurnCompletion()` on both success and error paths, plus targeted type-guard, ACP manager, ordering, and ACP dispatch transcript-safety tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not add `agent:turn:start`, new config, user-facing messaging changes, `CHANGELOG.md` edits, or broader lifecycle refactors.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #37833
- Related #60734
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: N/A
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Internal hook consumers can subscribe to `agent:turn:end` and receive a once-per-turn completion signal.
- The hook carries `sessionKey`, `success`, `durationMs`, and optional `errorCode` on failures.
- For ACP dispatch turns, transcript persistence completes before `agent:turn:end` is scheduled, so post-turn consumers can safely read the completed transcript. If transcript persistence fails, `agent:turn:end` is not emitted for that turn. Hook handlers are bounded fire-and-forget and are not awaited through later ACP state transitions or runtime cleanup.
- No user-facing messaging or config behavior changes.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[ACP turn completes] -> [latency/error stats only]

After:
[ACP turn completes]
  -> [latency/error stats]
  -> [optional dispatch transcript persistence callback]
  -> [agent:turn:end internal hook]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)

### Steps

1. Register an internal hook listener for `agent:turn:end`.
2. Trigger a successful ACP turn and a failing ACP turn through `AcpSessionManager`.
3. Verify the hook fires once per completed turn with the expected context and does not break the turn result.
4. Verify ACP dispatch persists the transcript before the manager schedules the hook.
5. Verify pre-hook completion failures, including ACP transcript persistence failures, suppress `agent:turn:end` instead of racing persisted state.

### Expected

- Success path emits `success: true` with `durationMs`.
- Failure path emits `success: false` and `errorCode`.
- Hook listener failures do not break the turn lifecycle.
- Dispatch-backed ACP turns persist transcript output before `agent:turn:end` is scheduled.
- If the pre-hook completion step fails, `agent:turn:end` is not emitted for that turn.

### Actual

- The contributor diff touches hook implementation, hook docs, ACP manager/dispatch code, and tests. It does not edit release-owned `CHANGELOG.md`.

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `agent:turn:end` fires after ACP turn completion on both success and failure, and the failure payload preserves `errorCode` (`ACP_TURN_FAILED`).
- **Real environment tested**: macOS local OpenClaw source checkout at `/Users/mantis/Projects/openclaw-60734-agent-turn-end-hook`, branch `feat/37833-agent-turn-end-hook`, using the production `AcpSessionManager` and internal-hook registry against an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`.

## Review Conversations

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Hook timing is important for post-turn consumers.

The docs avoid promising that fire-and-forget hook handlers are awaited through later state transitions or cleanup.

target.title: fix: suppress raw provider errors in channel delivery
target.body:
Fixes #69737.

## Summary

This change blocks raw unclassified assistant `errorMessage` text at the user-facing delivery boundary.

It adds a shared `formatUserFacingAssistantErrorText(...)` wrapper around the existing assistant error formatter. Classified friendly errors still use the existing sanitizer/classification behavior, but formatter output that is only raw upstream pass-through now falls back to:

```text
LLM request failed.
```

Terminal lifecycle events and reply payload construction now use the safe wrapper for actual assistant error turns. Raw diagnostic details are not globally deleted, provider-side error objects are not changed, and internal observation fields such as `rawErrorPreview` remain available for maintainers.

The follow-up repair preserves aborted-without-error behavior by keeping non-error aborted turns on the existing formatter path, so an aborted assistant with no `errorMessage` does not create a synthetic generic error payload.

## Evidence

- Synthetic canary: `SECRET_CANARY_69737`
- Current HEAD tested: `5bb04e6997717541b9c0ede4f0abf7784f49f06f`
- Raw provider-error suppression remains scoped to `stopReason="error"`.
- Aborted-without-error payload behavior is preserved.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Terminal assistant errors with unclassified raw upstream `errorMessage` must not send that raw text through user-facing lifecycle or reply payload delivery text. The repair also preserves aborted-without-error behavior so no synthetic generic error payload is emitted for aborted assistant turns with no `errorMessage`.

- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at HEAD `5bb04e6997717541b9c0ede4f0abf7784f49f06f`, running the actual `handleAgentEnd` lifecycle function and actual `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads` reply payload builder through `node --import tsx`, plus a live Telegram DM through my existing OpenClaw Telegram bot.

For Telegram, the PR checkout was started locally, `openai/gpt-5.5` was temporarily routed to a local OpenAI Responses-compatible endpoint at `127.0.0.1:8787/v1`, and a live Telegram DM triggered a provider 500 containing `SECRET_CANARY_69737`.

target.title: feat(status): detect externalized plugin version drift in --deep
target.body:
## Summary

Surface **externalized npm/ClawHub plugins whose installed version doesn't match the running gateway** in `openclaw [gateway] status --deep`. Pure diagnostic, deep-only, read-only — no behavior change anywhere else.

## Motivation

`openclaw update` already syncs plugins via `updatePluginsAfterCoreUpdate`. But `npm install -g openclaw@<v>` bumps the gateway binary only — the postinstall script intentionally avoids touching plugin packages:

> `scripts/postinstall-bundled-plugins.mjs`: *"Plugin package dependencies are installed only by explicit plugin install/update flows, never postinstall."*

That's a deliberate design choice (postinstall is hot, plugin installs need network and can be slow). The unfortunate consequence: a gateway updated via `npm install -g openclaw@<v>` continues running silently mismatched against its externalized plugins until someone separately runs `openclaw plugins update <id>`.

In practice this just bit a real-world user (myself) on the **2026.5.3 → 2026.5.4** transition: gateway moved to 2026.5.4, `@openclaw/whatsapp` stayed on 2026.5.3 in `~/.openclaw-<profile>/npm/node_modules/` for ~6 minutes before I happened to notice it via `openclaw plugins list`. Nothing in `gateway status` or `channels status` flagged it; both reported healthy/connected. Easy class of latent bug.

This PR closes that gap by surfacing the drift in `status --deep` — the same surface that already reports gateway event-loop health, channel transport state, port issues, etc. Adding "plugin X is on 2026.5.3 but gateway is on 2026.5.4" fits the existing shape.

## Behavior

`openclaw gateway status --deep` (or `openclaw status --deep`) now includes a new section when drift is detected:

```
Plugin version drift: 1 externalized plugin not on gateway 2026.5.4
- whatsapp: 2026.5.3 (npm) → expected 2026.5.4
Fix: openclaw plugins update <plugin-id> for each drifted plugin, then openclaw gateway restart.
```

When all plugins match, no extra output (silent). When run without `--deep`, no overhead at all. Bundled plugins (`origin: "bundled"`) ship with the gateway and are explicitly skipped — they cannot drift. Disabled plugins (`plugins.entries.<id>.enabled: false`) are also skipped since their version is not load-bearing.

## What this PR does **not** do

- ❌ No auto-update of plugins (deliberate; mirrors existing UX where channel/port issues are surfaced but not auto-resolved)
- ❌ No new CLI subcommand
- ❌ No change to the non-deep status path
- ❌ No new dependencies

## Files

- **`src/plugins/plugin-version-drift.ts`** (new, 120 LOC): pure `detectPluginVersionDrift()` helper. Compares each enabled externalized (`npm` / `clawhub`) install record against the gateway `VERSION`. Build-suffix qualifiers (`-1`) are normalized identically on both sides so `2026.5.4-1 ≈ 2026.5.4`.
- **`src/plugins/plugin-version-drift.test.ts`** (new, 172 LOC): 10 unit tests covering:
  - match / drift / suffix normalization
  - ClawHub source coverage
  - ignored sources (archive/path/git — those are operator-pinned local artifacts)
  - missing-version skip (cannot fabricate a comparison)
  - disabled-in-config skip
  - default-enabled-by-absence
  - deterministic alphabetical sort
- **`src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.ts`**: populate a new optional `pluginVersionDrift` field on `DaemonStatus`, only when called with `deep: true`. Failure to read install records is non-fatal — status renders normally.
- **`src/cli/daemon-cli/status.print.ts`**: render the new section when drifts exist, with a fix hint pointing at the existing `openclaw plugins update` command.
- **`src/cli/daemon-cli/probe.ts`**: (no net change after rebase) the daemon-cli probe wrapper already surfaces the running gateway `server`/`version` from the handshake; drift now consumes it.

_No `CHANGELOG.md` change — release-note context lives in this PR body per the repo's release-owned changelog policy._

## Verification

- ✅ `tsgo --noEmit -p tsconfig.core.json` — no errors in any touched file
- ✅ Pre-commit hooks pass
- ✅ `vitest run` on `plugin-version-drift.test.ts`, `status.gather.test.ts`, `probe.test.ts` — **49/49 pass** (includes 4 new gather regression tests)
- ✅ Rebased clean onto current `main`

## Review response (revision after ClawSweeper review)

Addressed all three findings:

- **[P2] Compare drift against the running Gateway version** — drift now uses the running gateway version resolved from the probe handshake (`rpc.server?.version` / `rpc.version`), falling back to the invoking CLI `VERSION` only when no gateway version is available. This removes the false-positive where a newer CLI probing an older still-running gateway flagged plugins that actually match the gateway.
- **[P2] Read install records from the daemon service environment** — `loadInstalledPluginIndexInstallRecords({ env: mergedDaemonEnv })` now reads the managed service's profile/state dir instead of the invoking CLI process's, so a CLI running under a different profile no longer produces missed/false drift.
- **[P3] Remove the CHANGELOG.md edit** — done; the branch no longer touches `CHANGELOG.md`.
- **Dirty branch** — rebased onto current `main`; conflicts resolved (the obsolete schema-snapshot workaround commit was dropped as it's no longer needed against current main).
- Added focused `status.gather` regression tests for the running-version and profile-mismatch cases.

## Why I think this belongs in core (not as a ClawHub skill)

This is filling a gap in an existing core diagnostic surface, not introducing new behavior. The same `status --deep` already reports gateway event-loop degraded states, channel transport issues, port conflicts, and stale gateway PIDs — adding plugin version drift fits that exact pattern. A ClawHub skill could implement the same detection externally, but the natural home for "is everything OK with my gateway?" is `openclaw status --deep`, where users already look.

Happy to adjust scope, naming, or output format based on review.



## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Externalized npm/ClawHub plugins whose installed version doesn't match the **running gateway** weren't surfaced anywhere. After review, drift is computed against the running gateway version (from the probe handshake), not the invoking CLI `VERSION`, and install records are read from the managed daemon service's profile/state dir. The detector still skips version-matched, explicitly-disabled, and non-externalized (path/bundled) plugins.

**Real environment tested:** macOS (Darwin arm64, Mac Studio), Node v25.9.0, pnpm 11.2.2. Branch `feat/plugin-version-drift-status-deep` rebased onto current `main` (head `e6074c9`), `pnpm install` completed. The real `detectPluginVersionDrift` module from `src/plugins/plugin-version-drift.ts` was imported and executed via `tsx` (no mocks), driven by a realistic install index. The CLI/Gateway version split and the service-env install-record read are additionally covered by real `status.gather` tests that drive `gatherDaemonStatus` with a mocked probe returning `server.version` and a daemon service env distinct from the CLI process env.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```

feat/plugin-version-drift-status-deep
$ git log --oneline -2

3358da8 feat(status): detect externalized plugin version drift in --deep
$ npx tsx proof-harness.mts                          # real detection module, bug-vs-fix
$ npx vitest run src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts \
                 src/cli/daemon-cli/probe.test.ts \
                 src/plugins/plugin-version-drift.test.ts
```

Scenario: invoking CLI is **2026.5.6**, the still-running gateway is **2026.5.4**. Install index: `@openclaw/whatsapp`=2026.5.3 (npm, behind running gateway), `@openclaw/discord`=2026.5.4 (npm, matches running gateway), `@openclaw/signal`=2026.5.2 (npm, but disabled in config), `context-topics` source `path`.

**Evidence after fix:** Live stdout copied from the run against the real module:


**Observed result after fix:** Under the old logic (CLI `VERSION` = 2026.5.6) `@openclaw/discord` was **wrongly** flagged as drifted even though it matches the running gateway. After the fix (running gateway version = 2026.5.4), only the genuinely-behind `@openclaw/whatsapp` is reported; `@openclaw/discord` (matches running gateway), `@openclaw/signal` (disabled), and `context-topics` (non-externalized `path`) are all correctly omitted — no false positives. **What was not tested:** End-to-end invocation of the live `openclaw gateway status --deep` CLI binary against a physically version-mismatched on-disk plugin install under a separate running gateway service. target.title: fix: remove isOpenAIProvider gate from applyPatchEnabled
target.body:
## Bug

apply_patch was unconditionally gated to only work with OpenAI providers (openai, openai-codex) via isOpenAIProvider(). This prevented the tool from being available on kimi, anthropic, qwen, stepfun, and all other providers.

## Root cause

In src/agents/agent-tools.ts, the applyPatchEnabled logic included a check that only allowed the tool for OpenAI providers.

## Fix

Remove the isOpenAIProvider check. The apply_patch format is client-side (*** Begin Patch / *** End Patch markers) and is not model-specific. The remaining isApplyPatchAllowedForModel check still allows per-model allowlisting via tools.exec.applyPatch.allowModels.

## Changes

- Removed isOpenAIProvider function definition (no longer needed)
- Removed isOpenAIProvider(options?.modelProvider) from applyPatchEnabled
- Updated test expectations in agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts to match new behavior

## Fixes

- Fixes #88357
- Closes #45269 (related — same underlying issue of apply_patch being treated as provider-specific)

## Verification

- Tool is now created for all providers when enabled
- isApplyPatchAllowedForModel still filters by allowModels if configured
- No functional change for OpenAI providers
- Tests updated and passing
- Branch merged with latest main, conflicts resolved

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** apply_patch tool was gated to only work with OpenAI providers (openai, openai-codex) via isOpenAIProvider(). This prevented non-OpenAI providers like kimi, anthropic, qwen, and stepfun from using the apply_patch tool even when explicitly enabled in config.

**Real environment tested:** OpenClaw gateway 2026.5.27 (27ae826) running on Linux (Ubuntu 24.04), agent:engineer session, kimi/kimi-for-coding provider (non-OpenAI).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
1. Started a new session with kimi/kimi-for-coding provider
2. Created a test file: echo "hello world" > test-apply-patch.txt
3. Invoked the edit tool (which uses apply_patch internally) to modify the file
4. Verified the file was modified correctly

**Evidence after fix:**
```
🦞 OpenClaw 2026.5.27 (27ae826)
🧠 Model: kimi/kimi-for-coding · 🔑 api-key (kimi:default)
⚙️ Execution: direct · Runtime: OpenClaw Pi Default

> edit test-apply-patch.txt
> Successfully replaced 1 block(s) in test-apply-patch.txt

$ cat test-apply-patch.txt
hello world
this is a test of apply_patch on kimi provider
```

**Observed result after fix:** The edit tool (which internally uses apply_patch) successfully modified the file on the kimi provider. The tool call completed without errors and the file content was updated as expected. This confirms apply_patch is now available and functional on non-OpenAI providers.

**What was not tested:** Direct apply_patch tool invocation via the raw patch format (*** Begin Patch / *** End Patch markers) — only tested through the edit tool wrapper. Other non-OpenAI providers (anthropic, qwen, stepfun, google) not explicitly tested, but the code change removes the provider gate universally.

target.title: fix(hooks): pass media metadata to internal message_received hook
target.body:
## Problem

Commit 65e2120f8c (PR #87297) added six missing media metadata fields
(`mediaPath`, `mediaUrl`, `mediaType`, `mediaPaths`, `mediaUrls`,
`mediaTypes`) to the **plugin** `toPluginMessageReceivedEvent` mapper,
but the parallel **internal** `toInternalMessageReceivedContext` mapper
was not updated.

Internal hooks registered via `registerInternalHook("message:received",
...)` receive a `MessageReceivedHookContext` whose `metadata` object is
built by `toInternalMessageReceivedContext`. Because the media fields
were omitted, internal hook consumers lose all media metadata on
received messages, even though the canonical context carries the data.

The plugin path and the inbound-claim path both surface these fields
correctly; only the internal hooks path is affected.

## Fix

Add the same six media fields to the `metadata` object in
`toInternalMessageReceivedContext`, matching the plugin mapper that was
fixed in 65e2120f8c.

Production diff is +6 lines in `src/hooks/message-hook-mappers.ts`, +8
lines of test assertions in the corresponding test file.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Internal hooks `message:received` context now
includes all six media metadata fields (`mediaPath`, `mediaUrl`,
`mediaType`, `mediaPaths`, `mediaUrls`, `mediaTypes`) in its metadata
object, matching the plugin path fixed in PR #87297.

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5, Node.js v22.16.0, OpenClaw built
